label: Self-supervised learning

node.label: Self-supervised learning
node.definition: Training targets are constructed from the data itself, such as masked regions, future tokens, or paired views.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Self-supervised learning is to connect Unsupervised learning, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units to downstream capabilities including Contrastive learning, Predictive representation learning / JEPA, In-context & test-time learning.
examples: substrate for Predictive representation learning / JEPA
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Unsupervised learning, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units.

dependencies.rationale: Self-supervised learning follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
Self-supervised learning uses Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Self-supervised learning uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Self-supervised learning uses Images, video & spatial grids as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Self-supervised learning needs sampled data batches to construct masking, pairing, or prediction targets from the observed data itself.
